global_context.scene_description: A cinematic editorial typography poster on a near-black matte backdrop. NO people, NO photographs, NO illustrations of objects. The entire image is bold, theatrical, art-directed typography. At the very top, a clean centered headline in bold uppercase white sans-serif: 'IT'S CHEAPER. FASTER. EASIER.' Below the headline, a small italic subhead in muted grey: 'Three myths Phoenix homeowners keep telling each other.' Below the subhead, a thin horizontal divider rule. Below the rule, a clean two-column comparison table with three rows. Column headers in small grey uppercase letterspaced text: 'WHAT YOU FEAR' on the left, 'WHAT IT ACTUALLY IS' on the right. The three rows: ROW 1 — left column: 'TIME' in small grey label above '2 WEEKS' in large faded grey struck-through bold; right column: 'TIME' label above '1 DAY' in massive bold white. ROW 2 — left column: 'MONEY' label above '$20,000' in large faded grey struck-through; right column: 'MONEY' label above '$250/MO' in massive bold white with a small green accent. ROW 3 — left column: 'HASSLE' label above '5 VISITS' in large faded grey struck-through; right column: 'HASSLE' label above '1 TRUCK' in massive bold white. Each row separated by a thin grey hairline. At the bottom, a single italic line in red accent: 'New AC install. The dread is the lie.' Composition is cinematic editorial poster — think a New York Times graphic, or an Apple keynote slide, but with theatrical contrast lighting and dramatic typography weight. Mood: cold confident mathematical correction. NOT a flat infographic, NOT corporate slide deck — this is editorial poster typography with theatrical depth. The near-black backdrop has subtle vignette falloff at the corners, the central content area is gently lit as if by a single overhead light.

lighting.quality: subtle vignette to dark corners, central content brightly lit

composition.framing: centered editorial poster typography, full-frame, balanced negative space
composition.focal_point: the three large white reality-numbers in the right column
